When you write code always consider clean code principles. Make sure to write code that is easy to read and understand. Use meaningful variable and function names, and avoid unnecessary complexity. Keep your functions small and focused on a single task. Use comments to explain why certain decisions were made, but avoid commenting on what the code is doing if it is already clear from the code itself. Always strive for simplicity and clarity in your code. When you write interfaces make sure to define clear and concise contracts for your functions and classes. Use type annotations to make it clear what types of data are expected and returned. Avoid using global variables and instead pass data through function parameters or class attributes. When working with APIs, make sure to handle errors gracefully and provide meaningful error messages to the user. Always test your code thoroughly to ensure that it works as expected and to catch any potential bugs or issues.
